summary: Win25H2Clean reversible probe confirmed EnableSecureCredentialPrompting=1 on the documented CredUI path and restored the baseline.

setting.name: Trusted Path for Credential Entry
setting.casual_explanation: This setting controls whether certain Windows credential prompts must happen on the Secure Desktop instead of inside the normal desktop session.
setting.why_people_change_it: People enable it when they want stronger protection against software that might try to capture credentials from normal desktop prompts.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Using the trusted path makes credential entry more secure for supported nonlogon prompts, but it can feel slightly less convenient.
setting.professional_notes: CredUI.admx and CredUI.adml document the policy name, path, and behavior for EnableSecureCredentialPrompting under HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\CredUI. The local ADML help text says enabling the policy requires credential entry on the Secure Desktop for nonlogon authentication tasks. The current app writes EnableSecureCredentialPrompting = 1, but this review record keeps that raw numeric mapping blocked until it is pinned down from an explicit primary source.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Users enter credentials within the normal desktop session for supported nonlogon tasks.

allowed_values.label: Current app value
allowed_values.meaning: Observed app write intended to require Secure Desktop credential entry for supported nonlogon tasks.

app_current_implementation.notes: Guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed the current app write and restore cycle for EnableSecureCredentialPrompting.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems supporting the CredUI trusted-path policy
windows_defaults.notes: The local ADML help text says disabled or not configured keeps credential entry inside the user's desktop session for supported nonlogon tasks.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
intended_for: Systems not explicitly hardening credential prompts with policy
avoid_for: Machines that require stricter Secure Desktop prompting

states.rationale: Leave the policy unset until the raw mapping is fully pinned down.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to keep the Windows baseline while this record is under review.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need an explicit trusted-path hardening policy today.
tradeoffs: Supported credential prompts can continue appearing within the desktop session.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app profile
recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting current app behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a fully validated raw policy mapping.
tradeoffs: Behavior is likely aligned with the official intent.
The explicit numeric mapping is still not documented cleanly enough for this dataset's validation bar.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft CredUI.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file documents the policy name, path, and value name for EnableSecureCredentialPrompting under the CredUI policy key.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft CredUI.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy requires credential entry on the Secure Desktop for supported nonlogon tasks, while disabled or not configured keeps prompts in the desktop session.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes EnableSecureCredentialPrompting = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: nohuto win-config mirror - EnableSecureCredentialPrompting policy
evidence.summary: CredUI.admx shows EnableSecureCredentialPrompting under HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\CredUI. The mirrored ExplainText says the policy requires trusted-path Windows credential entry on the Secure Desktop to prevent theft of credentials; disabling or not configuring it leaves credential prompts in the user session. The policy is explicitly called Require trusted path for credential entry.

decision.why: Win25H2Clean reversible probe confirmed EnableSecureCredentialPrompting transitions through missing -> 1 -> missing on the documented CredUI path.

validation_proof.notes: Guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean; see the batch probe output in H:\Temp\vm-tooling-staging\vm-batch-probe-20260320.json. Added nohuto mirror corroboration via nohuto-enablesecurecredentialprompting-admx.
